triageFlags.title: Credit Score Below Required Threshold
triageFlags.description: The applicant's credit score is below the minimum required for the proposed terms.
triageFlags.recommendation: Decline the application or consider alternative terms suitable for the applicant's credit tier.
triageFlags.reasoning: According to the policy, a credit score of 620 or higher is required for Tier B terms. The applicant's score of 575 places them in Tier C, necessitating stricter criteria.

triageFlags.title: Loan-to-Value Ratio Exceeds Maximum for Tier C
triageFlags.description: The Loan-to-Value ratio exceeds the maximum allowed for the applicant's credit tier.
triageFlags.recommendation: Decline the application or adjust the loan structure to comply with the LTV limit.
triageFlags.reasoning: The policy specifies a maximum LTV of 95% for Tier C applicants. The current LTV of 91.11% exceeds this limit, indicating a higher financial risk.

triageFlags.title: Debt-to-Income Ratio Exceeds Maximum for Tier C
triageFlags.description: The Debt-to-Income ratio exceeds the maximum allowed for the applicant's credit tier.
triageFlags.recommendation: Decline the application or reassess affordability to meet the DTI threshold.
triageFlags.reasoning: The policy sets a maximum DTI of 35% for Tier C applicants. The current DTI of 31.43% exceeds this limit, suggesting affordability concerns.

content.reasoning: The applicant's credit score places them in Tier C, which imposes stricter requirements. The Loan-to-Value ratio exceeds the maximum allowed for this tier, and the Debt-to-Income ratio also surpasses the policy limit. These factors collectively indicate a higher risk profile that does not align with the company's underwriting policies.
